channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Atiku Meets Rival Hayatu-Deen in Lagos as ADC Moves to Heal Post-Primary Rifts
item.description: Former Vice President and African Democratic Congress (ADC) presidential candidate, Atiku Abubakar, has held a closed-door meeting with his rival, Hayatu-Deen, at his residence in Lagos. The meeting i...

item.title: Renewed Beatlemania emerges in the UK ahead of Sam Mendes’ Beatles biopic series
item.description: The United Kingdom is experiencing a fresh surge of interest in The Beatles, with cultural commentators describing it as a new wave of Beatlemania ahead of a major series of biographical films directe...

item.title: Collingwood shed fire under investigation after no injuries reported
item.description: A small structure fire in Collingwood, Ontario, was brought under control without any reported injuries, according to local reporting. The incident involved a shed that caught fire in the early hours,...

item.title: Woman dies after entering sea in Thornton Cleveleys while attempting to rescue dog
item.description: A woman in her 60s has died after getting into difficulty in the sea while attempting to rescue her dog in Thornton Cleveleys, near Fleetwood Beach Cafe in Lancashire. According to Lancashire Police, ...

item.title: Analysis: Trump-linked America 250 celebration faces setbacks amid cancellations and controversy
item.description: A commentary piece examines the growing difficulties surrounding a multi-day U.S. 250th anniversary celebration project linked to President Donald Trump. The event, known as the Great American State F...

item.title: Fatal garbage truck crash closes westbound Highway 401 in Pickering, causing major traffic delays
item.description: A fatal collision involving a garbage truck on westbound Highway 401 in Pickering has led to significant traffic disruptions and is expected to result in a lengthy closure, according to the Ontario Pr...

item.title: SAHCO Staff Enjoy 40% Salary Boost as Union Applauds Management
item.description: The Air Transport Services Senior Staff Association of Nigeria (ATSSSAN) don give big commendation to Skyway Aviation Handling Company (SAHCO) management for their decision to increase salaries by 40%...

item.title: Thieves steal £6,200 worth of designer items from Shoreditch vintage shop during early morning raid
item.description: Two thieves carried out a burglary at Bread and Butter Collections, a vintage clothing shop in Shoreditch, London, in the early hours of 28 May. CCTV footage shows the pair arriving on bicycles at aro...

item.title: U.S. Evaluates Use of Cold War Plutonium Stockpiles as Fuel for Advanced Nuclear Reactors
item.description: The United States is reportedly exploring the possibility of repurposing plutonium recovered from dismantled Cold War-era nuclear warheads as fuel for next-generation nuclear reactors. The initiative ...

item.title: June 7 Webinar Highlights Global Protests Against Military Bases and Militarization
item.description: This article announces a scheduled online webinar focused on international activism against military bases and broader militarization. The event is presented as a two-hour session including presentati...

item.title: Veteran Tanzanian corporate leader Leonard Mususa dies at 72
item.description: Former Mwananchi Communications Limited (MCL) Board Chairman Leonard Clement Mususa has passed away at the age of 72, as confirmed by his family on Saturday, May 30, 2026. His death has been described...

item.title: Grace Mwai confirms separation from partner Kamuhunjia after years of relationship
item.description: Gospel singer Grace Mwai has officially announced that her relationship with her partner, Hiram Mwangi, popularly known as Kamuhunjia, has come to an end. In a public statement shared on Sunday, May 3...

item.title: West Coast Eagles secure convincing 30-point win over Essendon as rebuild shows progress
item.description: West Coast produced one of their most convincing performances of the 2026 AFL season, defeating Essendon by 30 points at Optus Stadium and continuing to show signs of meaningful progress in their rebu...

item.title: Pakistan reports limited progress in negotiations with Chinese power producers over tariff discounts
item.description: Power Minister Awais Leghari has said that Pakistan has not yet achieved meaningful progress in negotiations with Chinese power producers to secure tariff discounts under CPEC-linked power projects. H...

item.title: City of Johannesburg and National Treasury clash over R10.3bn municipal wage agreement amid funding threat
item.description: The City of Johannesburg is locked in a growing standoff with South Africa’s National Treasury over a controversial R10.3bn wage agreement signed with municipal workers. The deal, concluded last year ...

item.title: Bengal Governor Warns Against Undermining Public Trust After Election Results
item.description: West Bengal Governor R N Ravi, during an event in north Bengal, made remarks indirectly targeting the Trinamool Congress (TMC), cautioning that questioning democratic institutions after electoral loss...

item.title: Former ED investigator in high-profile Nirav Modi and Vijay Mallya cases takes voluntary retirement
item.description: Satyabrata Kumar, a senior Indian Revenue Service (IRS) officer and former Special Director of the Enforcement Directorate (ED), has opted for voluntary retirement from service (VRS), according to off...

item.title: Tanzanian Musician Malisa Reflects on Life Lessons Learned During 183 Days in Prison
item.description: Tanzanian musician Malisa recently opened up about her experience spending 183 days in prison, sharing both the emotional and physical challenges she faced. She described the harsh realities of prison...

item.title: Aranyak and the Early Literary Vision of Ecological Loss and Moral Conflict
item.description: The article examines Bibhutibhushan Bandyopadhyay’s 1939 Bengali novel Aranyak as a remarkably early literary exploration of ecological destruction and its emotional consequences. Set in the forests o...

item.title: Weekly Highlights from Moss & Fog: Wildlife, Nature Blooms, and Art Installations (May 25–31, 2026)
item.description: This weekly edition of Moss & Fog’s “Fog Signals” curates a selection of visually striking and unusual stories spanning nature, wildlife, and contemporary art. The issue highlights the aardwolf, a noc...
